com.hp.workpath.api.webservices

Class HttpResponse

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.webservices.HttpResponse

  • public class HttpResponse
extends java.lang.Object
    Class for accessing HttpResponse. This class provides methods for accessing the response status code, headers, the response body, and the HttpRequest corresponding to this response.
    Since:
    API 6

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      HttpResponse()
      Default constructor

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.Object getBody()
      Method to get request body
      java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> getHeader()
      Gets header value from the custom key
      java.lang.String getMethod()
      Getter for following 4 type of http method names GET - Used to request data from a specified resource. POST - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource. DELETE - Method deletes the specified resource. PUT - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource.
      int getStatusCode()
      Method to get status code of response
      java.lang.String getStatusDescription()
      Method to get status description
      void setBody(java.lang.Object body)
      Method to set request body
      void setHeader(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> header)
      Method to set header
      void setMethod(java.lang.String method)
      Setter for http method names like GET, POST, DELETE, PUT
      void setStatusCode(int statusCode)
      Method to set status code for http response
      void setStatusDescription(java.lang.String statusDescription)
      Method to set status description for http response

    • Constructor Detail

      • HttpResponse

        public HttpResponse()
        Default constructor
        Since:
        API 6

    • Method Detail

      • getHeader

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> getHeader()

        Gets header value from the custom key

        Returns:

        Header - value for the API header passed

        • Return can't be null since the method creates an empty hashmap for null header
        • Return can be empty if the value for header is empty

        Since:
        API 6

      • setHeader

        public void setHeader(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> header)

        Method to set header

        Parameters:
        header - Map of header key and value

        Sets header as a map of header key and header value pair

        Since:
        API 6

      • getMethod

        public java.lang.String getMethod()

        Getter for following 4 type of http method names

        • GET - Used to request data from a specified resource.
        • POST - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource.
        • DELETE - Method deletes the specified resource.
        • PUT - Used to send data to a server to create/update a resource.

        Returns:
        It will return the http callback method name. e.g: GET, POST, DELETE & PUT
        Since:
        API 6

      • setMethod

        public void setMethod(java.lang.String method)

        Setter for http method names like GET, POST, DELETE, PUT

        Parameters:
        method - Name of the method

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Instance variable assigned as empty for empty input
        • Instance variable assigned as NULL for null input
        • Expected input - GET, POST, DELETE, PUT

        Since:
        API 6

      • getBody

        public java.lang.Object getBody()
        Method to get request body
        Returns:
        Body of the request

        • Return can be null if the body is null
        • Return can be null if the body is empty

        Since:
        API 6

      • setBody

        public void setBody(java.lang.Object body)
        Method to set request body
        Parameters:
        body - request body

        Input should be the the body for the HttpResponse

        Since:
        API 6

      • getStatusCode

        public int getStatusCode()
        Method to get status code of response
        Returns:
        status code of the http response e.g: 200, 404, 400, etc

        • Return values conforming to the standards of http status code

        Since:
        API 6

      • setStatusCode

        public void setStatusCode(int statusCode)
        Method to set status code for http response
        Parameters:
        statusCode - status code for the http response

        No minimum or maximum values. It should be based on the HTTP response status code standard.

        Since:
        API 6

      • getStatusDescription

        public java.lang.String getStatusDescription()
        Method to get status description
        Returns:
        status description of the http response e.g: OK, Not Found, Bad Request, etc

        • Return can be null if the description is null
        • Return can be null if the description is empty

        Since:
        API 6

      • setStatusDescription

        public void setStatusDescription(java.lang.String statusDescription)
        Method to set status description for http response
        Parameters:
        statusDescription - Description for the http response

        • There is no minimum & maximum length
        • Instance variable assigned as empty string for empty input
        • Instance variable assigned as NULL for null input
        • Expected input - String value

        Since:
        API 6
